draft.title: What a photograph can prove
draft.learnerGoal: Separate visible observations from interpretations and catalog facts.

phenomenon.opening: Two viewers study the same city photograph but reach different conclusions about daily life.
phenomenon.question: Which conclusions are supported by the image itself?

draft.commitmentPrompt: Choose one claim and record how confident you are before inspecting the evidence.

plausibleReadings.label: The image proves the whole pattern
plausibleReadings.prediction: A single visible example is enough to establish how most people lived.

plausibleReadings.label: The image supports a bounded observation
plausibleReadings.prediction: The image establishes only what is visible at this place and moment.

separatingTest.setup: Compare a visible-detail claim, a catalog fact, and a broad historical inference against the image record.
separatingTest.whyItSeparates: The categories require different evidence and expose which claims the image alone can support.

explanationSections.heading: Observe
explanationSections.explanation: Describe only details that another viewer could point to in the image without adding a cause.
explanationSections.checkQuestion: Could another viewer locate every detail you named?

explanationSections.heading: Infer
explanationSections.explanation: Treat explanations and broad patterns as interpretations that need comparison with other records.
explanationSections.checkQuestion: What additional record would make this inference stronger?

draft.reconstructionPrompt: Write a rule for deciding when an image supports a claim and when another source is needed.

coldTransfer.prompt: Classify four claims about an unfamiliar photograph without hints, then justify one classification.
coldTransfer.successEvidence: The learner independently distinguishes four evidence categories on this task.
coldTransfer.remainsUntested: Delayed retention, other source types, and transfer across historical periods remain untested.

sourceNeeds.claim: The historical context and catalog description for each photograph
sourceNeeds.sourceType: Original archive catalog record and item rights statement

safetyNotes: Use public archival images and avoid asking learners to identify private people.
draftLimitations: No source has been checked by the model.
One cold transfer cannot establish mastery or delayed retention.

claimBoundary: AI-generated draft. Not a reviewed World, verified source record, grade, or proof of learning.
